note: Frozen stimulus for the surprisal study. Thirty single-turn 'Name a X' prompts, each a category with a WIDE space of valid one-word answers, identical for every model, no system prompt. The uniform one-word clamp is deliberate and differs from the convergence study's no-clamp rule: here the datum is the DISCRETE CHOICE (which answer), not the phrasing — the clamp is identical for all models so it cannot differentiate them, and it makes the metric verbosity-immune by construction (exact-match on normalized tokens, no embeddings, no judge). Metric: answer-choice surprisal = -log2 P(answer | all other models' answers), leave-one-out, add-one smoothed; companions modal-avoidance, novel-rate, self-distinctness. Bump spec_version if any category or the template changes.
